growth_records.cite: Jiang et al. Carbon Sources for Polyhydroxyalkanoates and an Integrated Biorefinery. International Journal of Molecular Sciences (2016). DOI:10.3390/ijms17071157. [PMC4964529]
growth_records.notes: REVIEW secondary value (Table 2, ref [45]). CDW 2.6 g/L, 60% P3HB, yield 0.11 g P3HB/g xylose. In-text (Young/Ramsay [45,46]): batch of 30 g/L D-xylose with N limitation gave 48-56% P3HB of CDW; max specific growth rates on xylose ~ similar to glucose (values not numerically given).

growth_records.cite: Jiang et al. Carbon Sources for Polyhydroxyalkanoates and an Integrated Biorefinery. International Journal of Molecular Sciences (2016). DOI:10.3390/ijms17071157. [PMC4964529]
growth_records.snippet: Glycerol | P. cepacia | 23.6 | 31.4 | P3HB | 0.6-1.5 | 0.062 [76]. After 120 h... yield of dry biomass was 23.6 g/L, of which 7.4 g/L was P3HB.
growth_records.notes: REVIEW secondary value (Table 2 + text, ref [76], Zhu et al., 400-L fed-batch, 120 h). CDW 23.6 g/L, 7.4 g/L P3HB (31.4%), yield 0.062 g/g. Table-2 productivity 0.6-1.5 g/L/h appears inconsistent with 7.4 g/L over 120 h (~0.06 g/L/h) -- reported verbatim, flagged.
